question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether departmental officials have conducted an impact assessment regarding fuel consumption and the switch to E10 petrol.
answer_text: Unfortunately, my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for an update on the development of the new Road Safety strategy; and (ii) whether it will reference road users having shared or hierarchy of responsibilities.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what work has been carried out by his Department to facilitate the creation of car sharing clubs.
answer_text: My Department is currently considering how cleaner transport, new business models and new modes of travel can transform how people, goods and services move, as part of our work in developing options for inclusion in our new suite of Transport Plans.Members of the public already have access to Car Club vehicles in several urban areas across the North. Additionally, a number of developers have recently included Car Clubs in their Travel Plans for residential developments in Belfast, and these are measures which are actively encouraged by my Department.I believe that the greater use of more efficient, on-demand transport schemes, such as Car Clubs, would help to enable better integrated services, help to reduce emissions, and ease congestion in our towns and cities. Virtually all Car Clubs across other jurisdictions operate as commercial businesses, and as such my Department has no plans to actively establish or operate them, I do however look forward to further operators entering the market and widening access to this service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the appointment of a replacement contractor for Priory and Springhill GP Surgeries.
answer_text: The Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) have initiated a process to find a new contractor to take over the Priory & Springhill GMS Contract from 1st February 2023.  There have been a number of expressions of interest put forward.  SPPG officers are currently engaging with potential new contract holders and I remain hopeful that one will be successful.  Whilst I am hesitant to provide a timeframe, I can confirm that SPPG officers are working hard to find a solution that will ensure that the full range of GMS services are provided to all registered patients at Priory & Springhill Surgery from 1st February 2023.  Patients and the wider public will be kept informed of any developments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 16250/17-22, to provide an up to date list of the requested road resurfacing projects in Ards and North Down Council area.
answer_text: I can confirm that the Department no longer maintains a list of requested road resurfacing projects within the Ards and North Down Council area. Resurfacing programmes in Ards and North Down are now, as in other areas, prepared on the basis of a number of factors primarily linked to road condition. Details of works programmes, including resurfacing schemes, for the 2022/23 financial year were included in the Department’s Council Report for Ards and North Down Borough Council. The Council Report was presented to Council on 11 October 2022 and is published on the DfI website and a link to the report issued to each MLA in the respective council area on 19 October 2022. See link; https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/annual-report-ards-and-north-down-borough-council-20222023Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to list the planning applications with his Department after being called in from district councils under the 2011 Planning Act.
answer_text: The Department currently has 13 live planning applications that were called in from local councils. These include applications for listed building consents that are associated with full planning applications. These are all listed below: 	Application Reference Number Site AddressProposal LA01/2017/0689/FLondonderry Arms Hotel, 39/41 Main Street, Portrush.Retention and part refurbishment/restoration of the structural walls, shop fronts and roofs of both No. 39 and 41 Main Street and demolition of the residual elements of the listed building within the full extent of the site at 39-41 Main Street and 2 Atlantic Avenue, Portrush, so as to erect a three to five storey, 83 bedroom hotel with a restaurant, cafe/bar and all associated ancillary services. LA01/2018/0446/LBCAs above As above, associated Listed Building Consent.LA09/2019/0024/OLands at former Maghera High School, Tobermore Road, Maghera. Mixed use and enterprise centre/ business units - which will be subject to Reserved Matters Planning Applications submitted by purchasers of plots from Mid Ulster District Council.LA09/2019/0025/FLands at former Maghera High School, Tobermore Road, Maghera.Proposed development of existing brown field site including new internal access road/footpaths, improved access, alteration of existing levels, utilities, site fencing, site clearance & associated site works for future mixed use and enterprise centre/business units.  LA11/2019/0263/FLandscaping and site works.  Kilfennan Link Roundabout, Crescent Link, Derry/Londonderry. Erection of a supermarket, associated car park, and amended access of approval A/2004/0879/F.LA07/2020/0788/F             Mourne Park House Mourne Park Kilkeel Newry BT34 4LBNew luxury hotel incorporating a spa, function room, restaurant, manager's quarters, associated facilities &car parking, by way of change of use & refurbishment of existing listed private residence and provision of a new build extension, gate lodge, associated site works & minor realignment of the existing access gates at the White Water entrance. LA07/2020/0843/LBCMourne Park House Mourne Park Kilkeel Newry BT34 4LBRefurbishment of and extension to existing fire damaged Mourne Park House and associated outbuildings to create a new luxury hotel comprising of hotel bedrooms, restaurant, function room, spa facilities, associated car parking and site works, Mourne Park Kilkeel.LA11/2018/0186/OLands due north of Holy Cross College & due south of Ardnalee Park, Strabane. Proposed multi-sport indoor/outdoor facility (circa 45,000 sq ft) incorporating wellbeing centre, community/youth facilities, full size indoor 6G pitch, fitness suite, sports hall, conferencing, office accommodation, exhibition and events space. LA05/2018/1154/OLands at Blaris Lisburn (lands between existing M1 Junction 8/ A101 roundabout and Moira Road/ Knockmore Road junction)Proposed mixed use development to include new housing (1300 dwellings) and commercial floor space (754,000 sq.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the (i) number; and (ii) total revenue, of non-domestic rates receipts derived in 2021-22 from childcare premises categorised on the non-domestic rating system.
answer_text: (i)	Rate assessments for non-domestic properties are predominantly based on the properties’ Net Annual Value.  The Rating system does contain valuation classifications suggesting childcare as the likely property use.  Also, this information is not gathered as part of the collection of rates.  As a result, it is not possible to give a precise number of childcare premises in the non-domestic rating system.(ii)	Childcare premises were within the class of businesses eligible for the Rates Holiday in 2021/22 and therefore paid no rates for the 2021/22 rating year.There may be properties occupied by childcare providers that have paid rates during 2021/22 which LPS is unaware of.  LPS is still accepting applications for the Rate Holidays from business owners who feel they were entitled but did not receive it.  Further information and a link to the online application is available at https://www.nibusinessinfo.co.uk/content/rates-holiday.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what assessment has been made of the impact a vacancy in the ministerial office post 28 October 2022 will have on departmental (i) decision making; and (ii) spending.
answer_text: The absence of Departmental Ministers will significantly hamper decision making, including decisions within my Department relating to strategic planning, major infrastructure projects and spending decisions relating to other key public services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the identified risks which could endanger the delivery of planned (i) network maintenance; and (ii) network developments, in the Ards and North Down Council Area by the end of 2022-23 financial year.
answer_text: My Department has identified a number of issues which present risks to the planned delivery of works across the Ards and North Down Council Area. As the contracts used are common to network maintenance and network development, the risks are similar for both areas of work. It should also be noted that the majority of risks are common to all Councils areas and are not limited to Ards and North Down.The key risk to delivery of planned works is contract availability and contract renewal preparations are progressing with award of an Asphalt Resurfacing Term Contract (which expired in November 2021) planned for January 2023 and award of a Term Contract for Environmental Maintenance (which expired in November 2021) planned for November 2022.  Contract renewal work for a Minor Works Term Contract (which expired in July 2022) is continuing, but a planned award date has not yet been confirmed. Any technical delay or challenge from an unsuccessful tenderer may impact on the availability of a contract on these dates.My Department has produced work programmes for the Council area which are included in the Annual Council Report, Annual Report to Ards and North Down Borough Council - 2022/23 (infrastructure-ni.gov.uk)Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action he is taking to secure the extension of the Energy Bill Relief Scheme beyond the initial six month period.
answer_text: I fully appreciate the plight of businesses who are experiencing unprecedented rises in energy costs and broader inflationary pressures driven by a perfect storm of internationally-driven factors. 	Much like the crisis created in the local economy as a result of Covid, the magnitude of this global inflationary crisis means that it can only be tackled by significant interventions at a national government level. As such, this is a policy which is being determined at a national level and the required financial resource is then being made available for delivery to all consumers across the UK.The Northern Ireland Executive simply does not have the levers or financial firepower to deliver the type of immediate help at the scale that is required, whether the Executive is fully functioning or not.  However, I believe that the certainty that the Energy Price Guarantee provides is essential for business and strongly believe that this should be extended by HMG.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what actions he is taking to ensure park home owners, who pay bills directly to the park owner and who are not on standard gas or electricity contracts, will be able to avail of the (i) Energy Price Guarantee; and (ii) Energy Bills Support Scheme.
answer_text: Park Home facilities are businesses and are therefore subject to all the relevant legal and regulatory requirements of a business. As a result, residents within Park Home facilities are not eligible for the UK Government’s domestic energy support schemes. 	The Park Owner, however, will receive support through the Energy Bill Relief Scheme: help for businesses and other non-domestic customers (Northern Ireland) - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk).	Draft legislation being introduced in Parliament this week includes conditions to ensure that support provided to businesses such as Park Owners is passed through to consumers who pay the park owner for electricity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the specific timescale for delivery of a controlled crossing at East Circular Road, Bangor, near St Columbanus' College.
answer_text: Local officials have confirmed that a controlled pedestrian crossing, which will improve road safety for pedestrians crossing this heavily trafficked A Class road, is included within a programme of works for this financial year.  Timescales for delivery of this scheme will be dependent on the availability of contractor resources.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at: dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what consultation and engagement has been undertaken with local people about the York Street Interchange Scheme following the successful procurement legal challenge that set aside the Contract Award.
answer_text: In November 2020 an Independent Assurance Review of the York Street Interchange scheme was carried out. Following on from this a Placemaking and Active Travel Review (PATR) of the scheme has been carried out by a specialist team independent from the project team. Both of these reviews engaged with key stakeholders, including Ashton Community Trust and Sailortown Regeneration, both of which represent communities in the area.Following publication of the PATR report on 11 October 2022 officials have contacted all of the stakeholders inviting them to take part in further engagement as options for the scheme are taken forward for consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the further actions and timescales for the York Street Interchange Scheme.
answer_text: I remain committed to the delivery of this strategically important scheme and the wider benefits it presents.On 11 October 2022 I published the report on the Placemaking and Active Travel Review, which has identified a number of potential options to improve placemaking and active travel provision as part of the project.  Further work is required to develop these options and consider factors relating to their potential implementation. Importantly, this will include further engagement with key stakeholders. This work is likely to take until early 2023 to complete and will inform next steps for the project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the planned spend during the current and future financial years on the York Street Interchange Scheme.
answer_text: In the absence of an Executive, budgets have not been agreed for the 2022-23 financial year or future years. However, to ensure that my Department continues to develop and deliver much needed investment in our infrastructure, I agreed capital allocations for 2022-23 which include £1.4m for the on-going development of the York Street Interchange project.   Future funding will be dependent on Executive priorities and associated budget settlements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the work that has been undertaken, in chronological order, relating to the York Street Interchange Scheme following the successful procurement legal challenge which resulted in the setting aside of the Contract Award.
answer_text: In December 2019 the procurement competition for York Street Interchange scheme was stopped and the contract award set aside, following a legal challenge. As a result of this, my Department has reviewed its procurement strategy in order to inform any future procurement competition.In July 2020 it was decided that a review of the scheme proposals should be carried out to provide assurance on how and to what extent the proposed scheme reflected and accommodated key objectives and priorities. This was an independent external assurance review, carried out in November 2020, and included engagement with key stakeholders. In March 2021 the recommendations from this review were published. Since then, work has been ongoing to address those recommendations including a further review of the scheme to ensure it aligns with the concept of placemaking and the latest best practice in terms of urban transport design. This Placemaking and Active Travel Review (PATR) was completed by a specialist team independent from the project team and, again, included engagement with key stakeholders. The review has identified options for further consideration to improve placemaking and active travel provision and work is now ongoing to refine these. I published the PATR on 11 October 2022 and it can be viewed at http://www.yorkstreetinterchange.com/

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how his Department monitors the number of Dental Surgeries in Northern Ireland that accept new NHS patients.
answer_text: The Business Services Organisation (BSO) has records of which General Dental Practitioners have a contract with the Department of Health to provide NHS treatment, and in which practices they work. However, the BSO does not have information as to the capacity of each GDP’s register, nor whether they are currently in a position to register new patients.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) how many dental surgeries in North Down are currently accepting new NHS registered patients; and (ii) where can patients find this information.
answer_text: Neither the Department of Health nor the Business Services Organisation (BSO) hold this information.The BSO have records of which General Dental Practitioners (GDPs) have a contract with the Department of Health to provide NHS treatment, and in which practices they work. The BSO does not have information as to the capacity of each GDP’s register.Patients can find a list of all General Dental Practices with at least one practitioner contracted to provide NHS treatment at http://online.hscni.net/other-hsc-organisations/hsc-service-finder. It can be used to search for dental practices in the patient’s area, sorted by those closest to their address and then extending further afield. A contact number for each dental practice is available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Belfast Distillery Company Ltd contract and works at Crumlin Road Gaol.
answer_text: Belfast Distillery Company Ltd has recently recommenced construction works to develop a Distillery and Visitor Experience at A Wing in the Crumlin Road Gaol. The company has advised that works are progressing as planned and the project is on target to complete by October 2023.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why public consultation has not yet commenced on the new draft Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan.
answer_text: My officials are currently continuing their preparations for a new suite of Transport Plans, intended to set out the framework for transport policy and investment decisions up until 2035. The Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan (RSTNTP) is the first Transport Plan in line for completion.  It is concerned with strategic roads, the buses which run on them and the rail network.I am keen to ensure that the measures proposed in the draft RSTNTP will be beneficial, sustainable and affordable as we seek to improve the regional transportation infrastructure in the next decade. As such I will publish the draft RSTNTP for public consultation when I am satisfied that all necessary work has been completed on it, and I have had an opportunity to fully consider it.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the strategy being developed to determine the most efficient mechanism to take forward A6 Phase 2 – Drumahoe to Caw.
answer_text: Part of the works associated with Phase 2 of the A6 Derry to Dungiven project, between Drumahoe and the Caw roundabout, will encroach on the Mobuoy waste site.  My Department is currently working closely with Northern Ireland Environment Agency (NIEA) and other stakeholders to ensure the final scheme design fully takes account of the impact of the waste site.  This integrated approach will ensure that any remediation proposals take account of respective parties’ requirements, including those relating to the A6 dualling.  The strategy for this phase of the project, which is not part of the current Dungiven to Drumahoe construction contract, will be informed by the remedial proposals for the Mobuoy site currently being developed by NIEA.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there are plans to include greater flexibility in the procedures to obtain permissions for the installation of cycling infrastructure on carriageways by external bodies.
answer_text: The provision of cycling infrastructure on the public road network by external bodies can be facilitated through the Planning process, and there are no plans to change this approach.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the timescales for delivery of an Active and Sustainable Travel Centre in the North West Multi-Modal Transport Hub.
answer_text: The Active Travel Centre officially opened on Wednesday 26th September 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on work undertaken following his predecessor's announcement in January 2022 to extend the Concessionary Fares Scheme.
answer_text: I understand the value that people place on concessionary fares and can see the benefit of extending free travel to those who previously received a half fare concession and making concessionary fares available on a wider range of public transport service.Officials are continuing to work on business cases to consider the extensions and have kept me fully informed of progress during the development process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what rights arise from the special share held by the Department in respect of Belfast International Airport.
answer_text: The Special Share arrangement my Department holds in respect of Belfast International Airport is set out in the Airport’s Articles of Association. It includes provision for Government to consider and approve any change in ownership of the Airport and to serve an Original Notice in the event of an Excluded Change of Control or a failure by the Airport to provide Key Operating Facilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance what preparatory work has been undertaken concerning the Public Bodies (Representation from Devolved Nations) Bill.
answer_text: This is not a matter for the Department of Finance.  The Executive Office (TEO) leads on this issue but cannot provide a response in the absence of its Ministers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the timescales for commencement and implementation of Reservoirs Act (NI) 2015.
answer_text: As you will be aware, my Department received statutory responsibility for the Reservoirs Act (NI) 2015 in June 2021. A number of sections of the Act commenced following Royal Assent in 2015; however, the remaining sections covering the main provisions of the Act can only commence through secondary legislation in the form of commencement orders and regulations, with the approval of the Assembly. It is anticipated that this will be progressed in the new Assembly Legislative Mandate and once complete my Department will then consider timescales for implementation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what support is available for women who suffered side-effects, the risk of which they were not made aware, from the use of the Essure implant device.
answer_text: Patients who are experiencing side-effects following Essure implant surgery should, in the first instance, consult with their GP and if necessary, seek referral to the Health and Social Care Trust where their surgery was performed, to discuss specialist care and treatment options based on their individual needs. For patients who are suffering chronic pain while awaiting further assessment and treatment, the Better Days Pain Support Programme is available to book online at hlcalliance.org. The programme includes face-to-face and remote programmes which are available in a number of locations across Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what advice was given to Trusts when the Essure implant device was withdrawn from the market in 2017 by its manufacturer, Bayer.
answer_text: Following the suspension of the Essure device’s CE mark on 3 August 2017, the Department advised the Health and Social Care Trusts on 18th August 2017 to suspend use of the Essure device and quarantine any remaining inventory. This was by means of an email from the Northern Ireland Adverse Incident Centre to Trust medical device liaison officers. The manufacturer’s Field Corrective Action to their customers at this time requested a voluntary suspension of use of the product and to temporarily quarantine any unused inventory until further notice.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the status of the Memorandum of Reply concerning the Public Accounts Committee report on Planning in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials are finalising a Memorandum of Reply to the 12 recommendations set out in the Public Accounts Committee report. As part of this process, there has been ongoing engagement with local government and other stakeholders. The Memorandum of Reply will be agreed with the Department of Finance Minister and submitted to the Public Accounts Committee when it is re-formed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will reply to my letter dated 3 June 2022 concerning the Roads (Miscellaneous Provision) Act (Northern Ireland) 2010.
answer_text: I am in receipt of the draft report on the review of the operation of the Roads (Miscellaneous Provision) Act (Northern Ireland) 2010, known as the Special Events legislation and I will respond to your letter once the report has been finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what efforts have been made to ensure that the Northern Ireland Planning Portal will not be offline for longer than the planned three weeks, while the service is moved to the new Portal.
answer_text: The regional project team has worked closely with a range of stakeholders: the incumbent supplier DXC Technology, the new IT supplier Terraquest Solutions and local government to minimise the impact of moving to a new Planning IT system. A transition plan has been agreed with relevant planning authorities that sets out the key activities in the run-up to and during the transfer to the new Planning IT System. The team have also completed a number of data transfers from the old Portal to ensure that this process is as short and robust as possible. While the transition period is estimated to be three weeks, the Northern Ireland Planning Portal will only be closed for one weekend and will then be available to provide read only access until the new System goes live.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the outcome of the NI Planning Portal User Survey will be published and whether the findings have been included as part of the specification for a new portal.
answer_text: The NI Planning Portal Survey was carried out June 2022 and the findings are currently under review by the regional Project Team. The key findings of this Survey will be published later this year after the implementation of the new Planning IT System.The specification for the new Planning Portal was developed in 2019 and so the findings of the recent survey could not have been included. However, the previous NI Planning Portal User Survey carried out in 2017 did inform the ‘Discovery’ stage of the Project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the timescales for achieving future milestones relating to the Review of Strategic Planning Policy on Renewable and Low Carbon Energy.
answer_text: The review of regional strategic planning policy for renewable and low carbon energy is ongoing. My Department distributed an ‘Issues Paper’ to targeted stakeholders in December 2021 for a period of 8 weeks consultation, which ended in February 2022 with over 40 responses received. In addition, a Strategic Environmental Assessment (SEA) Scoping Report was also issued to the statutory consultation bodies in June of this year, for the statutory 5 week period.The planning policy review is being progressed in accordance with established policy making best practice, in an iterative way alongside the draft Environmental Report being prepared by the SEA consultants. In doing so, account is being taken of the Executive’s Energy Strategy, the previous planning policy call for evidence, the independent consultant’s report that my Department commissioned, responses to the ‘Issues Paper’ and wider research. A draft revised policy document and draft Environmental Report are intended to issue for full public consultation later this year with all responses received being considered and the final policy published as soon as practicable next year, subject to Executive Committee agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the status of weed spraying in the North Down constituency including whether works are proceeding as originally scheduled.
answer_text: The weed spraying programme in Ards and North Down is well advanced with the initial treatment programmed to be completed by mid-August, subject to suitable weather conditions.While any schedule for weed spraying is always weather dependant, the works in Ards and North Down have progressed in line with expectations. Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 13669/17-22, for an update on the Driver and Vehicle Agency plans to move towards full acceptance of credit and debit card payments, removing cheques or postal orders as methods of payment.
answer_text: By way of update to AQW 13669/17-22, the Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) is progressing a programme of work to move towards the full acceptance of credit and debit card payments for the services it provides. Work that is currently underway includes the ability to apply online for a first provisional driving licence and the renewal of vocational driving licences for lorry and bus drivers.  It is anticipated these new online services will be implemented this financial year.  The DVA is also planning to introduce electronic payments to enable Approved Tachograph Centres to pay for their periodic approval inspection fees using credit or debit card payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 28606/17-22, whether his position remains the same as his predecessor concerning provision of a new safe off-road cycling path between Belfast and Bangor.
answer_text: As the member is aware the development of off-road cycling paths (greenways) is the responsibility of local Councils rather than my Department.  However I am keen to support the development of greenways and have recently invited councils to submit applications for grant in support of greenways and active travel generally.In relation to the development of a cycle path along the A2 Belfast Road there are significant challenges along the 2½ mile stretch of the A2 from Ballyrobert to Holywood.  This section consists of a four lane single carriageway road with narrow footways.  An initial general consideration of the route identified the following significant challenges to building a segregated cycle lane, which complies with up-to-date cycling design guidance:The vesting of a 3 – 4m strip of land along the length of the route involving numerous landowners;Design and safety considerations required to accommodate the numerous driveways that a cycle lane would have to cross; andSignificant level differences at various points along the route. These difficulties have not lessened since I took up the post of Minister for Infrastructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the work being undertaken by Sustrans to carry out an assessment of the current state of greenway development across Northern Ireland and to develop a phased programme for greenway delivery with councils.
answer_text: I have received the Sustrans reports on the current state of greenway development and the phased programme for greenway delivery.  I am considering the reports and the way forward for the future development of greenways across the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the timescale for delivery of the proposed flood alleviation schemes in the North West.
answer_text: My Department is developing a number of flood alleviation proposals for the North West.  Proposed schemes for Drumahoe and Eglinton have been progressed to detailed design. However, the development of these proposals are complex and have required re-evaluation to determine if a viable solution can be found. This re-evaluation continues and will also now include a review of the recent flooding to ensure that any proposals would be effective. It is hoped this re-evaluation will be completed by the end of 2022. Subject to the identification of a viable proposal the detailed design will be further developed, in conjunction with the environmental impact assessment, and I have asked my officials to progress this work as quickly as possible. A further flood alleviation scheme is currently proposed for the Derry City area, mainly focusing on tidal flood risk. It is intended this project will progress to design in the coming months. A feasibility study for Strabane is also on-going, which will assess the existing flood defences within Strabane.  Meanwhile, works to construct a short term flood alleviation scheme at Ballycolman will now commence on site this month as soon as contractor resource becomes available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he intends to revise his Department’s policy on 30mph speed limit repeater signs to take into account local road safety concerns.
answer_text: There are no plans to change the policy on the provision of 30mph repeater signs, which uses the presence of street lights to indicate that there is a 30 mph (48 km/h) speed limit, unless otherwise signed. This approach is well understood as in the 2019/20 Continuous Household Survey, 90% of respondents correctly identified that the presence of streetlights indicates that the speed limit is generally 30mph.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the timescale for the publication of the Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan.
answer_text: My officials are currently in the process of preparing a new suite of Transport Plans intended to set out the framework for transport policy and investment decisions up until 2035. The Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan (RSTNTP) is the first Transport Plan in line for completion.  It is concerned with strategic roads, the buses which run on them and the rail network.  I expect to publish a draft RSTNTP for public consultation when all work has been completed on it, and I have had an opportunity to fully consider it.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the methodology for the implementation of sectoral plans for transport which set a minimum spend of 10% on active travel from the overall transport budgets.
answer_text: The introduction of the Climate Change Act (NI) 2022 and the requirement for all Departments to set out policies and proposals within climate action plans to meet the 2050 net zero targets presents us with a real opportunity to address the climate emergency.My Department is working internally and collaboratively with DAERA in the development of the sectoral plans required by the Act including determining how the specific measures which set a minimum spend of 10% on active travel from the overall transport budgets can be met.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail how many children across Northern Ireland are in receipt of free school meals.
answer_text: The Education Authority has confirmed that at 30 June there were 102,577 children entitled to free schools meals.This is broken down as follows:Nursery Age - 4,213Primary Age - 54,032Post Primary Age (School Pupils) - 43,036FE Students - 1,296 Total 102,577

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline the (i) rationale; and (ii) consultation undertaken with key stakeholders, regarding the decision to change the date for the Modular exams for GCSE English and Maths from January 2023 to November/December 2022.
answer_text: Both January 2021 and January 2022 have been extremely difficult in regard to the public health context. Consequently, scheduling the exams for November/December reduces risk of cancellation.  My officials consulted with a wide range of educational stakeholders, including school leaders, young people and trade unions, to help inform my recent decisions for the awarding of CCEA qualifications in 2022/23. All post-primary leaders were also asked to complete an online survey on the proposed arrangements.  The majority of respondents were in favour or had no preference in regard to the re-scheduling of the winter GCSE series.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the timescale for updating the Highway Code.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Highway Code is the official guide for all citizens here with regards to using our roads safely and legally.  The code is periodically updated to take account of changes in legislation or new developments in the field of road safety.  As minister I take my responsibility for road safety very seriously, and have to consider the best way to use available resources to meet the needs of all road users, and to ensure that changes are managed in a way that they are clearly understood, reflect local needs and secure support from a wide range of road users.  I am therefore unable to give a timescale for the update of the Highway Code at this point.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what measures his Department is taking to ensure that groups which rely on the European Social Fund will receive match funding once the European Social Fund ends on 31st March 2023.
answer_text: As the Member will be aware, following the conclusion of EU funding programmes, my Department no longer has funding in baseline to continue provision of match funding. From 1st April 2023 the Shared Prosperity Fund is the sole funding source for activity currently supported under ESF. The Member should note that the Finance Minister and officials lead in negotiation of replacement EU Funds with UKG and we will continue to work with them and the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC) to try to ensure that funding is directed to priority areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he will consider further rates relief to help the hospitality sector following an increase energy and food costs.
answer_text: In the absence of an Executive I cannot bring forward any new rate reliefs for businesses.The cost of living crisis and the associated increasing costs to business is a matter of concern for all Executive Departments but without an Executive, no new cross-cutting fiscal policies can be introduced nor can the £435 million of available funding be allocated. This funding may be used to help businesses within the hospitality sector with the cost of living impact. Therefore, I hope an Executive can be reformed as quickly as possible to enable us to allocate these funds.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when his Department intends to bring forward a dedicated and bespoke Hospitality Strategy to help drive the sector's recovery following the COVID-19 pandemic.
answer_text: Hospitality cuts across a number of Executive Departments. My Department and the wider Executive implemented a wide range of support schemes to help drive the hospitality sector’s recovery following the COVID-19 pandemic.	I am pleased that the hospitality sector can this year look forward to a summer season free of any COVID related restrictions and I am hopeful for a 	positive summer.	Many of the topics that would need to be considered within a Hospitality Strategy are outside the remit of my Department, e.g. liquor licensing, use of outdoor spaces, pavement café licenses, opening hours. If Ministers agreed on the need for such a Strategy, it would therefore need to be a cross departmental piece of work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline whether his Department (i) maintains records of dropped kerbs and tactile paving across Northern Ireland; (ii) if this information is available to the public; and (iii) where this information can be accessed.
answer_text: My Department does not maintain records of the location of dropped kerbs and tactile paving.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 29290/17-22, whether any further consideration has been given to providing motorists with increased advance notice to book their MOT test, in light of the ongoing backlog.
answer_text: Pursuant to AQW 29290/17-22 and AQW 30994/17-22, Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) officials continue to monitor the Covid situation and will make a decision on when to increase the reminder notification period from six to seven weeks when they are satisfied that it has reached a more manageable position for certainty of staffing levels. The DVA has steadily increased its vehicle testing capacity to pre-Covid levels by adopting a range of measures including the recruitment of additional vehicle examiners, the use of overtime to provide cover for leave and by offering vehicle test appointments on a Sunday and bank holidays, when testing is not normally available. To minimise late cancellations due to the non-availability of staff, the DVA is offering more short notice appointments to ensure testing can be delivered when staffing availability is confirmed. This is allowing many customers to bring their MOT appointments forward, as new appointment slots are released.The DVA continues to experience significant demand for vehicle testing services and in some cases customers may not be able to have their vehicles tested before their current MOT certificate expires. In such cases, customers must book the earliest available test appointment for their vehicle, which may mean travelling to another test centre instead of their preferred location. The DVA releases vehicle test appointments on a daily basis and customers are encouraged to regularly check the booking system as they may be able to change their existing appointment to a different test centre and time if a slot becomes available. The DVA’s advice to vehicle owners who require an MOT to tax their vehicle and cannot secure a date before their MOT expires is to:book the earliest available appointment; andkeep checking the booking system for an earlier appointment.If they get to within five days of the expiry date of their vehicle tax and they have been unable to secure an MOT appointment or their vehicle tax has expired, they should contact the DVA Customer Services team at dva.customerservices@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk, and they will do their best to get an urgent appointment for the vehicle.  To date, all customers who have contacted the DVA in these circumstances have been offered an earlier appointment slot.The latest information on MOTs is available on NI Direct at https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/articles/book-view-change-or-cancel-mot-vehicle-test  and is also included as an advice note with every MOT Reminder Letter issued to their customers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) when the backlog in MOT testing will be fully addressed; (ii) what further measures his Department can take to enable people to access test appointments at their local testing centre before the expiry of their MOT certificate.
answer_text: The Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) has steadily increased its vehicle testing capacity to pre-Covid levels by adopting a range of measures including the recruitment of additional vehicle examiners, the use of overtime to provide cover for leave and by offering vehicle test appointments on a Sunday and bank holidays, when testing is not normally available.  The DVA is monitoring the number of applications received and tests carried out and expects waiting times to reduce over time through the actions they have taken to increase vehicle testing capacity across the network.The DVA continues to experience significant demand for vehicle testing services and in some cases customers may not be able to have their vehicles tested before their current MOT certificate expires.  In such cases, customers must book the earliest available test appointment for their vehicle, which may mean travelling to another test centre instead of their preferred location.  The DVA releases short notice vehicle test appointments on a daily basis to ensure testing can be delivered when staffing availability is confirmed.  Customers are encouraged to regularly check the booking system as they may be able to change their existing appointment to a different test centre and time if a slot becomes available. The latest information on MOTs is available on NI Direct at https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/articles/book-view-change-or-cancel-mot-vehicle-test and is also included as an advice note with every MOT Reminder Letter issued to their customers.The DVA will continue to work hard to reduce vehicle testing waiting times as quickly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when will the Annual Report confirming the road works programme for 2022/23 be presented to Ards and North Down Borough Council.
answer_text: Works programmes for the Ards and North Down area, as with all other Council areas, are now being finalised following recent confirmation of my Department’s 2022/23 contingency funding allocation. My Department’s officials will be in contact with their Council counterparts shortly to agree a date for the report to be presented. The Report will then be published on my Department’s website and placed in the Assembly library.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at: dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline an estimate of the financial costs of introducing an enhanced rider trainer scheme for motorcyclists in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Road safety is a matter of significant importance and I, as Minister for Infrastructure, am committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users, particularly vulnerable road user groups, including motorcyclists. There are a number of potential road safety interventions that were raised with the Department during the development of a new Road Safety Strategy, including an enhanced rider training scheme for motorcyclists. However, given the budget pressures faced by my Department for essential services, I am not in a position to commit any funding. My officials will continue to engage with all road user representative bodies as we continue our important work on road safety and on the delivery of a new Road Safety Strategy to 2030.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on his Department’s plans to introduce an enhanced rider trainer scheme for motorcyclists in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Road safety is a matter of significant importance and I, as Minister for Infrastructure, am committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users, particularly vulnerable road user groups, including motorcyclists. There are a number of potential road safety interventions that were raised with the Department during the development of a new Road Safety Strategy, including an enhanced rider training scheme for motorcyclists. However, given the budget pressures faced by my Department for essential services, I am not in a position to commit any funding. My officials will continue to engage with all road user representative bodies as we continue our important work on road safety and on the delivery of a new Road Safety Strategy to 2030.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the review of legislation in relation to byelaws governing Crawfordsburn Country Park and the surrounding areas, following feedback from Ards and North Down Borough Council.
answer_text: I can confirm that my officials are currently reviewing the content of feedback from Ards and North Down Borough Council and have sought legal advice from the Departmental Solicitor’s Office (DSO).  On full consideration of this advice, my Department will make further contact with the council in relation to the byelaws in place which cover Crawfordsburn Country Park and surrounding areas.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how many scheduled roads resurfacing projects in North Down, which were included in his Department's 2021/22 Annual Report, have not been completed due to the legal challenge of the asphalt resurfacing contract competition.
answer_text: Five schemes in the planned programme for 2021/22 were not completed prior to the expiry of the asphalt resurfacing contract in November 2021 at: Hawthorne Drive, Bangor; Beechwood Avenue, Bangor; Beechwood Gardens, Bangor; Silverstream Drive, Bangor; and Kinnegar Drive, Holywood.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at: dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when his Department will update the remit of the Utility Regulator to include decarbonisation.
answer_text: The Utility Regulator operates under an extensive legislative and regulatory framework and is required to exercise its functions in line with a broad range of duties. These include an obligation to exercise its functions in a way best calculated to secure a diverse, viable and environmentally sustainable long-term energy supply, which includes the need to guard against climate change. The extent to which new or amended frameworks will be needed to support the delivery of the Executive’s ‘Path to Net Zero Energy’ Strategy is being assessed by my officials, in conjunction with the Utility Regulator, as an integral part of ongoing policy work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy why the Path to Net Zero Energy Strategy did not include the socialisation of connection charges for (i) electric vehicle chargers; and (ii) new domestic heating systems such as heat pumps.
answer_text: The Energy Strategy Path to Net Zero and the Action Plan sets out a long term, high level vision of net zero carbon and affordable energy which includes electrification of heat and transport.Delivering these ambitions will require a range of supporting workstreams to be undertaken and delivered, including reviewing connection charging policy particularly in the context of low carbon technologies such as electric vehicles and heat pumps.  The Utility Regulator has commenced the connection charging review and expect to publish a call for evidence later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23732/17-22, for an update on the implementation of Article 8 of the EU Energy Performance of Buildings Directive.
answer_text: The minimum requirements for electric vehicle charging infrastructure were originally set out in Article 8 of the European Union’s Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (EPBD) for new residential and new and existing non-residential buildings. Following the British Government’s indication of its intention to comply with the Directive, transposition of the EPBD into legislation, is a devolved responsibility.My Department is currently engaging with officials from other departments here and across these islands to examine the most effective way to bring forward the provision of EV Charging Infrastructure in dwellings into legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 320/22-27, to outline the timescales for resolving issues uncovered by GP practice visits.
answer_text: The Directorate of Integrated Care, SPPG, is undertaking a schedule of practice visits incorporating assessment, support and development to all General Medical Practices.  The Practice Visit covers key contractual areas and also addresses Practice specific queries, concerns and issues.As part of the visit process, if issues are identified, an action plan is agreed that details the issues discussed, actions agreed to address, and timelines for completion.  The action plan is added to a GMS visit report and shared with the Practice but the contents of the report are confidential between SPPG and the GP Practice and therefore, the information is not published.  There is often a range of different issues identified through the GMS visit process with different timelines agreed to resolve, depending on the nature of the issue.  It is therefore not possible to accurately outline the timescale for resolving issues uncovered at GMS Practice visits.If issues are identified through the GMS visits process, SPPG colleagues work with GP practices to ensure that they are resolved in a timely manner, according to agreed timescales appropriate to the issue at hand.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether consideration has been given to setting up a mutual bank in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials have been in regular contact with stakeholders wishing to establish a mutual bank over the last couple of years regarding their ongoing campaign. My officials continue to be willing to engage with the promoters of a mutual bank in developing their proposals. Also, there have been requests to make legislative changes so that a mutual bank can offer a full range of services. There was not sufficient time within the previous Assembly Mandate to complete these changes; therefore, this issue can be considered in the new mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when his Department will make public the broad findings about ongoing service and accessibility issues with regard to General Practice in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Primary Care services have been under sustained pressure for a number of years prior to the pandemic. GP-led services cannot continue to meet the rising demand and expectations without urgent action to address the pressures facing the service. I am aware that some patients are experiencing difficulties in relation to access to GP services. My Department has established a Working Group to explore issues around access to GP services, including demand for services, limitations of infrastructure, and staffing and resourcing issues. The Working Group will also consider how technology can be better used to provide improved access in a standardised way across Northern Ireland. Coming out of this, the Working Group will propose actions to be taken in the short, medium and long term to improve access for patients.The Group includes representatives from General Practice, service users and officials from my Departmental among its membership.The Working Group met for the first time on 1 June and there is no timescale as yet for when it shall report. When it does so, my Department will consider the findings and recommendations of the Working Group and respond as appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why grass cuttings are not lifted from treated areas.
answer_text: As part of the Department’s current verge management approach there is no need to lift grass cuttings, however we do lift cuttings in our ‘Don’t Mow Let It Grow’ areas. The Department has been engaged in the ‘Don’t Mow Let It Grow’ pilot project in conjunction with Causeway Coast & Glens Borough Council, Northern Ireland Environment Agency and others, in support of the All-Ireland Pollinator Plan, which contains a specific action ‘to promote pollinator-friendly management of the national road network in NI’. Details of this project can be found at: https://dontmowletitgrow.com/. Going forward I am keen that our grass cutting policy supports this plan more widely and consideration is being given to lifting grass cuttings in more areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 22140/17-22, for an update on the review of Residents’ Parking Schemes including timescales for publishing the review report.
answer_text: I can confirm that the review of residents’ parking schemes is complete and I plan to make the report public once I have had the opportunity to discuss its findings with my officials.  The review report will set out the lessons learnt from the Rugby Road scheme in Belfast, and the other schemes that did not make it to implementation, and will be used to establish the policy for such schemes going forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what action he is taking to address the impact of inflation on the tapered annual allowance concerning the pensions of medical professionals.
answer_text: The current high rate of inflation is having a significant impact on all staff employed in the HSC as well as those who have now retired. The impact is also reflected across all areas of society. Taxation is of course a reserved matter, with Annual Allowance (AA) limits set by HMT.  I do however recognise the issues regarding taxation rules on pensions and am also aware that the current high rate of inflation is likely to exacerbate the issue in the near future. Departmental officials are considering the issues associated with this together with colleagues in England, Wales and Scotland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has given consideration to the publication of a Driver and Vehicle Agency capital investment plan which will facilitate the reintroduction of a full emissions test for cars and light goods vehicles under 3,500kgs.
answer_text: The Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) currently conducts a fully compliant diesel emissions test on all heavy goods vehicles, buses and vans over 3,500kgs, and a partial diesel emissions test for cars and light goods vehicles. This includes a visual inspection of the vehicle’s emissions and a check of the engine Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL), which indicates a defect in the vehicle emission control systems.The reintroduction of a full emissions test for cars and light goods vehicles under 3,500kgs, will require a substantial capital investment in the vehicle testing estate to create a safe environment for staff and customers, in which an emissions test for diesel cars and light goods vehicles can be conducted. As a first step, and as part of the DVA’s ongoing modernisation programme, it is investing in the construction of new test centres at Hydebank in Belfast and Mallusk in Newtownabbey. These new test centres include facilities for full emissions testing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when his Department intends to resume roadside grass cutting to the standard of five times per year.
answer_text: In support of the All-Ireland Pollinator Plan, which contains a specific action ‘to promote pollinator-friendly management of the national road network in NI’, the Department has been engaged in the ‘Don’t Mow Let It Grow’ pilot project in conjunction with Causeway Coast & Glens Borough Council, Northern Ireland Environment Agency and others.  Details of this project can be found at: https://dontmowletitgrow.com/. Going forward I am keen that our grass cutting policy supports this plan more widely.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline what action his Department is taking to address the erection of flags without permission on street furniture owned by his Department.
answer_text: Article 87 of the Roads (NI) Order 1993 makes it an offence to attach unauthorised signs or advertisements to structures such as lamp posts. One of my Department’s primary considerations is the safety of the public and where unauthorised flags or attachments pose a risk to road users, my Department will seek to remove that hazard. Where there is no such danger, my Department will liaise closely with other key stakeholders and seek to provide a solution.I note the commitments to respect for different cultures and identities contained in the New Decade New Approach. Finding a sustainable solution to the ongoing problem of illegally erected flags and emblems requires leadership and a united approach. I can assure you that I am committed to working with Executive colleagues and all stakeholders to deal with this issue comprehensively.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether consideration has been given to the rollout of a carer's allowance supplement in Northern Ireland, similar to that introduced in Scotland.
answer_text: I appointed an independent panel to complete a comprehensive review of existing welfare mitigation measures and to consider a future mitigation package. 	The panel engaged with a wide range of people who access the social security system and with key stakeholders (including carers) so that their voices are heard on these important issues	The implementation of any new payment schemes will require Executive agreement on the allocation of necessary funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how the Energy Bills Support Scheme can be rolled out in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I refer you to the AQW 1038/22-27 from Mr Doug Beattie MLA on 08/06/2022 where this question was previously answered.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when he will consult on legislation that will allow couples to convert civil partnerships, conducted outside Northern Ireland, to marriages.
answer_text: Conversions of civil partnerships conducted outside of the jurisdiction is a complex area of law, which would require careful consideration and agreement with other jurisdictions. It is possible that it could be considered as part of future policy considerations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the Translink bus and rail (i) passenger numbers; and (ii) ticket sales revenue for the months of (a) February 2019; and (b) February 2022.
answer_text: Details regarding Translink’s bus and rail (i) passenger numbers; and (ii) ticket sales revenue for February 2019 and 2022 are an operational matter for Translink. You should, therefore, direct your question to Translink at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether consideration has been given to the rollout of a carer's allowance supplement in Northern Ireland, similar to that introduced in Scotland.
answer_text: Carer’s allowance is the responsibility of the Department for Communities and it would be for that Department to consider.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he intends to publish the outcomes of the Health and Social Care Board's GP practice visits.
answer_text: The Directorate of Integrated Care, SPPG, are undertaking a schedule of practice visits incorporating assessment, support and development to all General Medical Practices.  The Practice Visit covers key contractual areas and Practice specific information.  The outcome of Practice Visits are private and confidential between the SPPG and GP Practices, and therefore this information is not published.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he intends to publish the report from the independent investigation into asphalt resurfacing procurement, led by Sarah Hannaford QC.
answer_text: My officials are currently working through the procurement of interim resurfacing contracts, whilst developing a longer-term delivery strategy for resurfacing and other road work activities.  It would not be appropriate to publish the report until after this work has been completed. This is likely to be towards the end of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether consideration has been given to granting domestic rates relief to people participating in the Homes for Ukraine scheme.
answer_text: Sponsors participating in the Homes for Ukraine scheme are entitled to a £350 per month ‘thank you’ payment, funded by the British Government, which is intended to cover overheads. Any change in regard to rate relief would necessitate a change in the legislation governing domestic rate relief, and this cannot be progressed without a functioning Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the following asphalt resurfacing contracts will be awarded (i) IAW1 Magherafelt; (ii) IAW2 Omagh; (iii) IAW3 Dungannon; (iv) IAW4 Strabane; (v) IAS1 Newry and Mourne; and (vi) IAS2 Down.
answer_text: The current asphalt resurfacing procurement strategy consists of four phases with six new term contracts in each.   The assessment of tenders for the first phase, which includes contracts for Magherafelt, Omagh, Dungannon, Strabane, Newry & Mourne, and Down, is well underway with contracts anticipated to be awarded in June 2022.
